subject: Statistics
title: Earthquake Magnitudes
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Earthquake Magnitudes.

hook.text: A magnitude 7 quake is 10× stronger than a 6, and 100× stronger than a 5. Power grows fast.

buildup.text: Earthquake magnitudes follow the Gutenberg-Richter law: a power law. Small quakes happen constantly.

discovery.text: For every magnitude 7 quake, there are roughly 10 of magnitude 6 and 100 of magnitude 5.

twist.text: This means devastating quakes are rare but guaranteed. The distribution promises them.

climax.text: Japan builds for extreme quakes because the power law says they will come. Not if — when.

punchline.text: Power laws don't predict when. They promise it will.

quiz.question: What does the Gutenberg-Richter law describe?
options: Earthquake frequency follows a power law — small quakes are common, large ones are rare but inevitable
Earthquakes happen at random with no pattern
Large earthquakes are more common than small ones
